assertion_results.text: Impact map or task descriptions explicitly flag at least 3 ambiguities from the feature description
assertion_results.evidence: The impact-map.md 'Ambiguities Identified' section explicitly lists 5 ambiguities: (1) 'Search should be faster' — no performance baseline or target defined, (2) 'Results should be more relevant' — no definition of relevance, (3) 'Add filters — some kind of filtering capability' — filter dimensions not specified, (4) No specification of which entities are searchable, (5) Non-functional requirements are unmeasurable. All 5 exceed the minimum of 3.

assertion_results.text: Plan acknowledges that 'Better UI' (non-MVP) cannot be planned without design mockups or frontend repository and excludes it from scope
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md 'Out of Scope' section states: '"Better UI" (non-MVP): This requirement is explicitly marked as non-MVP in the feature description. Additionally, the target repository (trustify-backend) is a Rust backend service with no frontend code. No frontend repository is available in the Repository Registry, and no Figma design mockups have been provided. This requirement is excluded from the implementation plan entirely.'

assertion_results.text: Tasks still follow the task-description-template.md format despite ambiguous requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 task files contain the required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Modify (and/or Files to Create), Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Task 1 also has Reuse Candidates and Verification Commands. Tasks 2-4 include Dependencies. All sections follow the format prescribed in task-description-template.md.

assertion_results.text: Tasks document assumptions where they fill in missing details, labeled as assumptions pending clarification
assertion_results.evidence: The impact-map.md documents assumptions for each ambiguity using the explicit label 'Assumption (pending clarification)'. Examples: Ambiguity #1 states 'Assumption (pending clarification): We assume the primary performance bottleneck is the lack of database-level full-text search indexes.' Ambiguity #2: 'Assumption (pending clarification): We assume relevance means PostgreSQL full-text search ranking (ts_rank)...' Ambiguity #3: 'Assumption (pending clarification): Based on the entity models visible in the repository... we assume filters should cover entity type, advisory severity, and package license.' All 5 ambiguities have labeled assumptions.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 task files contain '## Repository' followed by 'trustify-backend' as a single repository name. Task 1 line 1-2: '## Repository\ntrustify-backend'. Task 2 line 1-2: same. Task 3 line 1-2: same. Task 4 line 1-2: same. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 tasks contain all 4 required sections. Task 1: '## Target Branch' (line 4), '## Description' (line 7), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 41), '## Test Requirements' (line 49). Task 2: '## Target Branch' (line 4), '## Description' (line 7), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 38), '## Test Requirements' (line 46). Task 3: '## Target Branch' (line 4), '## Description' (line 7), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 44), '## Test Requirements' (line 56). Task 4: '## Target Branch' (line 4), '## Description' (line 7), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 65), '## Test Requirements' (line 74).

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the repo-backend.md mock repository structure manifest, not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: All Files to Modify paths exist in repo-backend.md manifest: Task 1: 'migration/src/lib.rs' (manifest line 18). Task 2: 'modules/search/src/service/mod.rs' (manifest line 91), 'modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs' (manifest line 93). Task 3: same two search module paths. Task 4: 'tests/api/search.rs' (manifest line 113). Task 1's Files to Create path 'migration/src/m0002_search_indexes/mod.rs' follows the exact pattern of the existing 'migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs' (manifest line 20) within the migration/src/ directory structure.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 Implementation Notes reference: 'migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs', 'entity/src/sbom.rs', 'entity/src/advisory.rs', 'entity/src/package.rs', 'migration/src/lib.rs' — all from manifest. Task 2 references: 'modules/search/src/service/mod.rs', 'common/src/model/paginated.rs', 'common/src/db/query.rs', 'common/src/error.rs', 'modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs', plus specific code patterns like 'plainto_tsquery', 'ts_rank', 'PaginatedResults<T>', 'Result<T, AppError>'. Task 3 references: 'modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/package/model/summary.rs', 'common/src/db/query.rs', 'common/src/error.rs'. Task 4 references: 'tests/api/search.rs', 'tests/api/sbom.rs', 'tests/api/advisory.rs' with specific patterns like 'assert_eq!(resp.status(), StatusCode::OK)'.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section set to 'main'
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 tasks have '## Target Branch\nmain'. Task 1 lines 4-5, Task 2 lines 4-5, Task 3 lines 4-5, Task 4 lines 4-5.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a real computed SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters, not a placeholder (<hex-digest>, <hex>), abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256:<64-char-hex>'
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 tasks end with a valid digest line. Task 1: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256:92fbddbb47633eb2c409b2aaafb0027169dd80a07471002d857c71dba7ba094f' (64 hex chars). Task 2: 'sha256:b6323382cef1c580239746e8b931675feb2e79dffd2ca05afdebc16afd9f7a16' (64 hex chars). Task 3: 'sha256:01be2819c556f56e659518385ac90860e9e6dd9a23d586934e7077345e5f473c' (64 hex chars). Task 4: 'sha256:a7979fa8ae2384832196f95a73417bb85a2d9b77c47fcff112804f3f386c555c' (64 hex chars). All are exactly 64 lowercase hex characters, no placeholders or abbreviated values.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: No convention applicability rationales in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>') appear anywhere in any of the 4 task files or the impact map. Grepping for 'Applies:' and 'Per CONVENTIONS' returned zero matches across all output files. The repo-backend.md manifest indicates a CONVENTIONS.md file exists in the repository root, but the tasks' Implementation Notes reference 'docs/constraints.md' sections (e.g., '§2.1', '§2.2', '§2.3', '§5.1') without any convention-applicability validation or rationale in the prescribed format. Convention-aware enrichment with file-type applicability checking was not performed.
